name: Chocolate Spice Bundt Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F and butter (do not flour) a 10-inch bundt pan.
2. Cream together the butter and sugar until well combined. Beat in the eggs one at a time, ensuring all are incorporated.
3. Add the melted chocolate and mix until thoroughly blended.
4. In a separate bowl, sift together the dry ingredients: flour, cardamom, salt, and baking soda.
5. Alternately add the flour mixture and sour cream to the chocolate mixture, beating well after each addition until the batter is very smooth.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared bundt pan, spreading evenly with a rubber spatula.
7. Bake at 325°F for 45-50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 5 minutes. Then turn the pan upside down and carefully transfer the cake to a rack to finish cooling.
Ganache Glaze:
1. Heat the cream in a heavy saucepan until just below a boil.
2. Add the chocolate and stir constantly with a wooden spoon until it is completely melted and well combined with the cream.
3. Allow the ganache to cool until warm, then pour generously over the cooled cake, letting it drip attractively down the sides.
4. If the glaze thickens as it cools, refrigerate for 30 minutes to set quickly.
5. The glaze can also be used to sandwich cookies or as a cake filling and keeps well at room temperature. Cover the cut edge with wrap.
